I haven't actually bought this item, yet.

But I was THINKING about buying it, today, which is kind of an example of the law of unintended consequences. I will explain:

I park my car outside, because up until recently, one-half of our garage was filled with junk. I didn't INTEND it to be filled with junk, it just ended up that way, because a few years back, our city changed the way garbage pickup worked. It used to be that you could put pretty much any old thing out at the curb for the trash pickup. You'd have your garbage cans, sure, but if you, say, broke a kitchen chair you could put that out there and they'd just pick it up and take it. Couch cushions, old drawers, whatever it was that broke around the house, you could throw away, even if it didn't fit in the can.

That was because back then, garbage men got out of the truck and picked up the can and dumped it in the truck, so they could also pick up your old chair or floor lamp or novelty statue, too. So that worked great, except for the time we had a tree branch fall on our plastic garbage can and break it, so that the garbage can was dented permanently and cracked and no good for its intended purpose of holding garbage.

Oh lord this thing has some controversy around it! Just like one of the top reviews of this product mentioned, I myself was a little cautious about this little generator. Here's a MAJOR tip to prevent any of the failures the more negative reviews mentions, please read the manual! It specifies everything that the generator CAN and CANNOT do! I repeat, this generator cannot save the entire world! But it can save parts of yours! That rant out of the way, let's get into the products strengths and weaknesses in a fairly unorganized review. (Sorry ahead of time!)

Strengths and Weakness:
= In general it is a economically efficient, jack of all trades, master of none type of deal. Yes it has many helpful features, but they aren't as great as tools DEDICATED to that one specific task. For example, the air-compressor to re-inflate tires or inflatable beds. A dedicated air-compressor would do both of those things relatively quickly. While the Rally Generator can inflate those two things, it won't be as fast as the dedicated air-compressor. In other words, it is what it is. It CAN do those things. Just not as quickly as tools dedicated to that one and only specific task.

= Again with the "master of none" type of situation we have going on here, the battery capacity, for a generator (granted, a very low priced generator, under $100 USD is very impressive actually!

Both of my cargo doors (aka suicide doors) on my 2006 Dodge Dakota SLT Extended Cab truck failed. Even though the description did not say that it was for my truck, I searched the web and found that it was indeed what I was looking for to fix my problem. The weak plastic ends for the two cables (4 total for both doors) that control the release of the upper and lower door latches crack and break and because of this, it won't allow the latches to release properly. If you only have 1 broken door cable end now, I HIGHLY HIGHLY HIGHLY recommend you do both doors now (all 4 cables) and save yourself the headache and pain later on with having another door you cannot open easily.

While I would consider this failure to be a safety issue and SHOULD be a recalled item by the NHTSA, it won't be even though it affects SO SO SO many vehicles! I mean, you CANNOT open the doors when the weak plastic ends break off, and eventually they will. At that point, anyone in the back seat cannot get out of the vehicle quickly and easily as they should be able to do. It is CLEARLY a design flaw using such a weak inferior component to secure the cables in place and the automotive manufacturers should look somewhere else to save a few pennies when making the cars. But you know, save that nickel no matter how much it may cost you later, eh? Bean Counters and Lawyers......yeesh!
top_rant.title: Exactly what I needed for my 2006 Dodge Dakota SLT Extended Cab truck

I USE COMPRESSED AIR ON THE PULLEYS AND LINKAGES. AND BLOW THE LEAVES AND DIRT OFF. THE PULLEY BEARINGS ARE NOT WATERPROOF. NOT SEALED AND THE BEARINGS WILL RUST USING HIGH PRESSURE WATER. SINCE I SWAPPED TO AIR THE PROBLEMS STOPPED. I REMOVE THE SPINDLE BEARINGS EVERY FALL, WASH OUT OLD GREASE AND HAND PACK AND REINSTALL PLASTIC COVERS. I HAVE GOTTEN 5 YRS AND STILL SUPER SMOOTH. THE SPINDLE FITTING IS A FRAUD. AND IT WILL USE UP AN ENTIRE TUBE OF GREASE. IF YOU REMOVE THE INSIDE COVERS AND PUMP GREASE INTO SPINDLE HOUSING THE FORCE OF PRESSURE WILL BLOW OUT THE OUTER BEARING COVERS. THEN THEY WILL FAIL. THE BEARING COMES OUT WITH A LIGHT TAP OF A SCREWDRIVER AND EASY TO PUT IN. KEEP BLADES SHARP AND BALANCED. I'VE USED HIGH LIFT AND MULCHING BLADES AND REALLY NO DIFFERENC

Installation of this exhaust was super easy. I get leery every time I see the "insert the flared end over the standard diameter pipe" connections on exhausts like this vs flanges that bolt together, but the flared sections were great on this exhaust. I was fortunate enough to be able to do this on a lift, and I wasn't able to get the large mid-section off without cutting it. If you're doing this with the truck on jackstands there's no way in h3ll you'll be able to get it off in one piece since it hasn't to be angled so far down to work the back section over the rear axle.

I spent so much time trying to get the damn exhaust hangers off (my truck had 8000 miles on it when I did this, so the rubber was nice and new and sticky on the exhaust hangers), and yes, I bathed them in PB Blaster, which did help. Do yourself a HUGE favor and buy an exhaust hanger removal tool and save tons of time. top_five_star_obscene.text:
The HOTOR car trash bin is wonderful! I just gave it to my mom as a Christmas gift for her brand new 2021 RAV4 Premium. Her interior is the black leather, and this trash bin is black too, so it will blend in.

It is durable. Well made. The stitching looks firm and reliable. The bin has a plastic lining, so you could put ice in it, to keep a couple of drinks cold if you wanted to. It came with 2 trash bags, and they are held in the bin by 4 strong little magnets that are like snaps. The bin is just the right size, and the strap that holds it in the car fit perfectly around her Toyota's headrest. I placed it behind the driver's seat, so I can reach it easily.

I think the best thing about this bin is the top lid though. It has a pretty neat lid that has an opening where you can put your trash though it. The opening has 4 flanges that stay closed after your put your rubbish in it. That is the best part! You don't have to actually go and open the lid-- you just put your trash through the opening, and it keeps everything from spilling out!! How clever of the manufacturer to include this convenient feature!

The lid also has a velcro area that keeps it snugly closed at all times. This is just what my mom really wanted for a gift, a trash can for her brand new Toyota, to keep it nice and clean. I am very very glad I purchased it from HOTOR
top_five_star_obscene.title: A well made, durable, and useful trash can for Mom's new car!

I have always struggled with what to do with that tissue or candy wrapper while driving and so it would get shoved in my cup holder. Well then the cup holder would be filled with trash, look dirty, and be a pain to dig it all out when I got to a trash can. Well this little trash can was the perfect option. I can open the lid, dispose of my trash, and then close the lid leaving it all out of sight. Then when I get to a trashcan I can just grab this container, open the lid, and dump it into the trash.

My only complaint with this trash can was that because of the flared sides it didn't sit well in some of my cup holders that have spring loaded buttons that help hold a cup in place. The springs were strong enough that it would try to push this can out when empty. My solution was to just use the cup holder on my car door that did not have these springs and it worked out better because it opened up a cup holder in the center console. Overall I am very happy as this really keeps down on the little bits of trash that used to accumulate in my car.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I had a back-of-the-seat trash container, but it was almost impossible to line it with a used grocery bag. I had to take the whole thing out of the car to empty it, and that was just too annoying. Every time I had to do that, I thought that it must have been designed by apes!

Enter this intelligently designed trash container! This company has devised a clever way to grip used grocery bag’s handles so they cannot get pushed to the inside, which makes it a snap to empty and to re-line. In addition, it has several pockets on the outside of the trash container to hold necessaries, like more used grocery bag liners, a bottle of hand disinfectant, and more. When you take it out of the box, you will need to insert a strip of stiff plastic into a channel at the top of the bag; it takes a bit of hand muscle, but my weak old hands accomplished the task in less than two minutes. This stiff plastic strip forms the half moon shape of the bag, and makes it simple to stuff trash into the bag. This very cool trash receptacle is big enough to hold more than a whole day’s traveling trash. If the leakproof liner becomes soiled, just pull it out and take it to the sink. Easy/peasy! I am entirely satisfied with this cleverly designed automotive trash containment device, and I am happy to recommend it to friends and family members.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Intelligently designed / easy to install / easy to use / easy to empty / attractive / EXCELLENT!

It's high quality, is already double bagged, waterproof and uses standard grocery bags with handles, which are clipped on at the sides. The pockets are perfect to hold extra bags. So much better!
